name: multi-function-type-change
instruction:
Add a `priority: Int` field to the `Task` type (after `done`). Then update all functions:
1. `pending` — also filter out tasks with `priority < 1`, and sort results by `priority` descending (sort_by priority, then reverse)
2. `done_count` — no logic change, but must still compile
3. `summary` — change format to `"${title} [${status}] p${priority}"` where priority is converted to string
4. `find_task` — no logic change, but must still compile
5. `mark_done` — when constructing the new Task record, preserve the `priority` field from the original task
layer3_hint: Available: Record type fields: `type Task = { title: String, done: Bool, priority: Int }`. Record construction: `Task { title: t.title, done: true, priority: t.priority }`. `list.filter(xs, fn(x) => bool)`, `list.sort_by(xs, fn(x) => key)`, `list.reverse(xs)`. `int.to_string(n)` converts Int to String. `and` for logical AND. `${}` for string interpolation.
